summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined a series of specific developments in small UAS operations and countermeasures. Ukraine has reportedly reduced Russian FPV drone activity by 18% in February through targeted strikes on logistics 50 km behind the front in Donetsk, while Ukrainian engineers have developed the Chipa net gun to physically snare FPV drones when other air defenses fail. Russia is rapidly expanding its drone force to 101,000 troops by April, and an Iran-backed militia has claimed its first-ever FPV drone strike on a US base in Baghdad, releasing video evidence. Additionally, Russian forces tested a white “penguin” camouflage suit in Ukraine, but at least two soldiers wearing it were quickly neutralized by FPV drones in a snowy landscape.
